notes: These evals exercise kite-research against a tiny self-contained fixture codebase (files/*.ts) plus a code-blind plan fixture (files/plan-public-profiles.md) whose scenarios reference capabilities that EXIST in the fixture, are MISSING from it, and one whose design assumption the fixture flatly contradicts. The skill must answer every research question one-to-one, point EXISTS findings at a function/module with a file:line and a reuse note, point MISSING findings at a concrete extension point, record reuse constraints as caveats, raise a BLOCKING finding (status blocked) where the codebase contradicts the plan, put only names/locations (never copied source) in the plan, and update each scenario's status to researched or blocked.

evals.name: full-plan-research-with-blocking
evals.prompt: Here's a kite-planner plan file (files/plan-public-profiles.md) and the codebase it targets (the other files/*.ts in this folder — treat them as the real repo: src/users/service.ts, src/routes/account.ts, src/middleware/auth.ts, src/orders/schema.ts, src/notifications/notifier.ts). Research the codebase and answer every scenario's research questions, then annotate the plan file and update the statuses.
evals.expected_output: An annotated copy of the plan file in which every research question across S1, S2, and S3 has a one-to-one finding. S1 answers are both EXISTS: requireAuth in the auth middleware and getUserById in the user service, each with a file:line and a reuse note, and getUserById's 'assumes caller already authenticated' caveat recorded as a reuse constraint. S2 has one MISSING finding (no username lookup) and one MISSING finding for the public route, each naming a concrete extension point. S3 surfaces a BLOCKING finding because the design demands a synchronous order-confirmation send with an inline delivery result but the codebase only has an async, queue-only notifier and the order schema has no email field; S3's status becomes blocked. S1 and S2 statuses become researched. No source code is copied into the plan file.
expectations: Every research question in the plan is answered one-to-one: S1.RQ1, S1.RQ2, S2.RQ1, S2.RQ2, S3.RQ1, and S3.RQ2 each have a corresponding finding, with none left unanswered
S1.RQ1 is answered EXISTS, naming requireAuth in the auth middleware (src/middleware/auth.ts) with a file:line location and a note on how to reuse it
S1.RQ2 is answered EXISTS, naming getUserById in the user service (src/users/service.ts) with a file:line location and a note that it returns the full user record and can be reused directly
The reuse constraint that getUserById assumes the caller is already authenticated is recorded as a caveat on that EXISTS finding
S2.RQ1 is answered MISSING (no lookup-by-username exists) and names a concrete extension point — where to add the username lookup (e.g. alongside getUserById in src/users/service.ts)

S2.RQ2 is answered MISSING (no public/unauthenticated profile route exists) and names a concrete extension point — where to add a new public route fitting the existing routing structure (e.g. a profile route alongside src/routes/account.ts)

S3 raises a BLOCKING finding: the design requires a synchronous order-confirmation send returning an inline delivery result, but the codebase's notifier is asynchronous/queue-only (queueOrderConfirmation) and the order schema has no email field, so the plan's assumption is contradicted
S3's status is set to blocked, and the BLOCKING finding explains exactly what is wrong rather than inventing a workaround
S1 and S2 statuses are updated to researched (and the scenario order/status table reflects researched/blocked, not still planned)
Only names and locations appear in the annotated plan — no verbatim source code from the fixture files is copied into the plan

evals.name: single-scenario-exists-and-missing
evals.prompt: I'm only interested in the 'View someone's public profile' scenario (S2) in files/plan-public-profiles.md right now. Check what we already have in the codebase (the files/*.ts in this folder) for looking up a user by username and for serving a public profile route, and write the findings for that scenario into the plan. Don't paste any of our source into the plan — just tell me where things are.
evals.expected_output: Findings written for S2 only. Both research questions answered one-to-one as MISSING: there is no username lookup (the existing getUserById is by id, so the answer should note that and point to where a username lookup would be added) and there is no public unauthenticated route (the existing account routes are auth-gated, so the answer names where a new public profile route should live). The plan contains pointers and file:line references but no copied source code. S2's status becomes researched.
expectations: Both of S2's research questions are answered one-to-one (S2.RQ1 and S2.RQ2), neither left unanswered

S2.RQ1 is MISSING — recognizes that the existing getUserById looks up by id, not username, so no username lookup exists — and names a concrete extension point for adding one (e.g. in src/users/service.ts next to getUserById)

S2.RQ2 is MISSING — recognizes the existing routes (src/routes/account.ts) are authenticated/account-scoped, so no public profile route exists — and names where a new public unauthenticated route should be added to fit the routing structure
Findings cite file:line locations for the existing code referenced (getUserById, the account routes) rather than vague references
No verbatim source code from the fixture is copied into the plan — only names and locations
S2's status is updated to researched

evals.name: blocking-contradiction-detection
evals.prompt: Focus on the order-confirmation scenario (S3) in files/plan-public-profiles.md. The design says checkout must send the confirmation email synchronously and return the delivery result in the response. Look at how notifications and orders actually work in the codebase (files/notifier.ts and files/order-schema.ts, plus the rest) and tell me whether the plan can be implemented as written. Annotate S3 and set its status.
evals.expected_output: A BLOCKING finding on S3. The notifier (queueOrderConfirmation) is asynchronous and queue-only with no synchronous path that returns a delivery result, and the Order schema has no email field, so the design's synchronous-send-with-inline-result assumption cannot be satisfied. The skill must NOT invent a workaround; it records the BLOCKING finding describing exactly the contradiction and sets S3's status to blocked, signaling kite-planner must re-plan. Both of S3's research questions are still answered one-to-one, and no source code is copied into the plan.
expectations: Both of S3's research questions (S3.RQ1 and S3.RQ2) are answered one-to-one

S3.RQ1 establishes there is no synchronous order-confirmation send that returns an inline delivery result — only the asynchronous, queue-only queueOrderConfirmation (src/notifications/notifier.ts) exists — cited with a file:line
S3.RQ2 establishes the Order schema (src/orders/schema.ts) has no email field to send to, cited with a file:line

A BLOCKING finding is raised on S3 explaining the contradiction precisely: the design assumes a synchronous send with inline delivery result and an order email field, neither of which the codebase supports
The skill does NOT invent a workaround or redesign the solution — it surfaces the contradiction as a blocker for kite-planner to re-plan
S3's status is set to blocked
No verbatim source code from the fixture is copied into the plan — only names and locations
